channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Minister Kombo urges Tanzanian students abroad to uphold discipline and strengthen the nation’s global image
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has reaffirmed its commitment to valuing Tanzanian students studying abroad as key contributors to the nation’s socio-economic growth and international presence. During his ...

item.title: How automation is reshaping retail forex trading behaviour
item.description: Retail forex trading is undergoing a significant behavioural shift driven by the rise of automation tools. Traditionally, many retail traders relied heavily on constant screen time, instinctive reacti...

item.title: Saint Augustine’s African Roots and Influence on Pope Leo XIV
item.description: Pope Leo XIV recently began his first official visit to Africa with a stop in Algeria, where he visited the Grand Mosque of Algiers to strengthen Christian-Muslim relations and pay tribute to Saint Au...

item.title: Moya Brennan, iconic Irish singer and Clannad lead, dies aged 73
item.description: Irish Grammy-winning singer Moya Brennan, celebrated as the lead vocalist of Clannad and sister of globally renowned Enya, has passed away at the age of 73 in Donegal, surrounded by her family. Known ...

item.title: Superquiz for Wednesday, 15 April: puzzle feature and editorial correction
item.description: This article presents the latest edition of the Superquiz, published for Wednesday, 15 April, as part of the Sydney Morning Herald’s regular puzzle offerings. The Superquiz encourages readers to engag...

item.title: RCMP Confirms Two Separate Shootings in Grand Falls-Windsor Involved Intimate Partner Violence
item.description: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) have confirmed that two recent shootings in Grand Falls-Windsor, Newfoundland and Labrador, were incidents of intimate partner violence. Superintendent Paul Pe...

item.title: Alex Eala bows out of Stuttgart Open following defeat to Leylah Fernandez
item.description: Filipino tennis player Alex Eala was eliminated from the Stuttgart Open after facing Leylah Fernandez in a challenging match. Eala, who has been making waves on the international tennis scene, put up ...

item.title: Omah Lay claims top spot in Afrobeats artistry, releases new album 'Clarity of Mind'
item.description: Nigerian singer Omah Lay don again yan say na im be di greatest Afrobeats artist for di last ten years. For interview with Capital XTRA, e talk say even if people fit dey look am for numbers, personal...

item.title: Brian Kemp urges unity among Republicans amid party divisions
item.description: In a recent Politico video feature, Georgia Governor Brian Kemp outlined what he calls a 'big tent' approach to revitalize and unify a Republican Party struggling with internal divisions. Kemp, who ha...

item.title: Man lynched in Mansa over alleged extramarital affair
item.description: In a shocking incident from Mansa, Punjab, a man was reportedly tied to a tree and lynched by a group of people due to an alleged relationship with a married woman. Local authorities have confirmed th...

item.title: Albanian gang sentenced for extensive burglary operations totalling £163.1 million
item.description: A major Albanian criminal gang has been handed lengthy prison sentences after being convicted for a series of high-value burglaries across the UK, totalling an estimated £163.1 million. The gang was i...

item.title: Nexford University launches Fellowship to train young Nigerians for global careers with affordable online U.S. degrees and guidance from African leaders
item.description: Nexford University don launch one major initiative for African youth wey dem call the NextGen Africa Workforce Fellowship. The program na pan-African one designed to equip young people for today’s glo...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Continues Hearings into Allegations of Corruption and Political Interference in Justice System
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry has continued its hearings into serious allegations involving criminality, political interference, and corruption within South Africa’s criminal justice system. The ...

item.title: Challenges Facing Young People in England’s Coastal Communities Despite Green Job Growth
item.description: Despite the UK government's push for a green energy revolution along England's east coast, young people in coastal towns like Lowestoft and Great Yarmouth are struggling to access promised opportuniti...

item.title: Boiler tube explosion at Chhattisgarh power plant leaves four dead and 15 injured
item.description: A serious industrial accident took place in Chhattisgarh’s Sakti district on Tuesday when a boiler tube exploded at a power plant operated by Vedanta Limited in Singhitarai village. According to preli...

item.title: Adamawa APC governorship aspirant resigns from party over lack of internal democracy
item.description: Maurice Vunobolki, wey be governorship aspirant for Adamawa State, don officially resign from the All Progressives Congress (APC), citing serious concerns about lack of transparency, fairness, and int...

item.title: Popes Through History Who Addressed Political and Social Issues
item.description: The article discusses how several Popes throughout history have openly spoken about political matters, often taking moral stands against policies and actions that contradict the principles of human ri...

item.title: Thirteen police recruits arraigned over alleged KCSE certificate forgery case
item.description: Thirteen police recruits have been taken to court in Nairobi after they were accused of using forged Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education (KCSE) certificates to secure employment within the Nation...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Political Influence in Kenya’s IPO Market and Capital Market Governance
item.description: The article discusses growing concerns about the integrity of Kenya’s Initial Public Offering (IPO) market, following remarks attributed to KIHAERU Member of Parliament Ndindi Nyoro. He has warned tha...

item.title: High Court orders Musa Khawula to apologise and retract defamatory statements about Julius Malema and his wife
item.description: The South Gauteng High Court in Johannesburg has ruled against gossip columnist Musa Khawula following defamatory claims he made about EFF leader Julius Malema and his wife, Mantoa Matlala-Malema. In ...
